headline: AT&T, IBD Stock Of The Day, Near Buy Point After Surging On Earnings
summary: AT&T is the IBD Stock of the Day as the telecom leader's relative strength line improves amid volatility in artificial intelligence stocks and the U.S.-Iran war as some investors rotate into high dividend paying companies.  Shares of AT&T stock have gained about 16% in 2026 after advancing 9% last year.  On the stock market today, AT&T shares dipped nearly 2% to 28.41 in afternoon trading, but were still up slightly for the week.

headline: NYSE fined $9 million by SEC over glitch that disrupted stock market
summary: NEW YORK, March 6 (Reuters) - The New York Stock Exchange has agreed to pay a $9 million ‌civil fine to settle U. Securities and Exchange ‌Commission charges over a computer glitch that disrupted the stock market open ​in January 2023, causing wild swings in the prices of blue-chip stocks.

headline: Is It Time To Reassess Verizon (VZ) After Its Strong Year To Date Share Price Run?
summary: If you are wondering whether Verizon Communications shares still offer value at current levels, this article will walk through what the numbers say about the stock’s price. With the share price at US$51.18, Verizon has returns of 4.7% over 7 days, 10.7% over 30 days, 26.3% year to date, 23.6% over 1 year, 71.6% over 3 years and 25.2% over 5 years. This raises the question of how that lines up with its underlying worth. Recent attention on Verizon has focused on its position as a major US...

headline: AT&T vs. Verizon in 2026: Which Telecom Dividend Stock Is Actually Worth Owning?
summary: Verizon suffered a major network outage this week, leaving hundreds of thousands of customers without service. A jarring reminder that in telecom, reliability is the whole product. It raises a fair question for dividend investors in either of these giants: is the yield you’re collecting built on a foundation that’s getting stronger, or one that’s ... headline: Verizon Weighs V2X Growth Opportunities Against Sponsorship Cost Controls
summary: Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ) is working with Aptiv and Wind River on a proof of concept for vehicle to everything safety technology showcased at MWC Barcelona. The project uses Verizon's 5G and edge computing to support real time sensor data sharing between vehicles and surrounding infrastructure. At the same time, Verizon is reviewing major marketing and sponsorship deals, including its long running NFL partnership, as part of broader cost controls under new leadership. For investors...
